seoTitle: Port Melbourne vs Portarlington — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Melbourne and Portarlington. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Melbourne vs Portarlington: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Melbourne and Portarlington.
heading: Port Melbourne vs Portarlington — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Melbourne, Victoria and Portarlington, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Portarlington has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Port Melbourne (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Melbourne or Portarlington safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Melbourne has a flood risk score of 42/100, while Portarlington scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Melbourne scores 49/100 for bushfire risk. Portarlington scores 15/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Melbourne or Portarlington?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Portarlington has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
